name: pad
description: Mehrabian-Russell PAD affect space as a 3-D pleasure x arousal x dominance box (A. Mehrabian & J. A. Russell, 'An Approach to Environmental Psychology', 1974). Extends the bundled `circumplex` manifold with the dominance (potency/control) axis the 2-D Russell model leaves out. The nine V-A plane nodes are circumplex's first-person mood corpora reused verbatim at dominance 0 (the D=0 equator), so the PAD plane is exactly the circumplex disk; six added nodes vary dominance: `dominant`/`submissive` are the pure +/-D poles, `furious`/`fearful` place anger and fear at their PAD-correct dominance signs, and `triumphant`/`humiliated` anchor the +P+A+D and -P-A-D corners (pride vs defeat) so the high- and low-dominance half-spaces are constrained across valence, not only at negative valence. furious (-P+A+D) and fearful (-P+A-D) share valence and arousal and differ only in dominance, so they isolate the new axis. This folds the bundled affect vectors into one surface: happy.sad is the pleasure chord, angry.calm runs through `furious`, fearful.unflinching through `fearful`, and authoritative.submissive is the dominance axis itself, the donor the other two only sampled obliquely (cf. the documented angry.calm <-> authoritative.submissive entanglement, anger encoded as dominance). Authored, not discover-mode: the coordinate system is declared, so the same (pleasure, arousal, dominance) point is steering-comparable across model families and users.
